title: Extract shared scheduler test harness to tests/helpers.py
description: Move `FakeRunner` and the reusable `OrchestratorTests` base class into `tests/helpers.py`, updating scheduler-oriented tests to import the shared harness from there.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler-focused tests can import shared fixtures from `tests/helpers.py`.
The extraction does not change test behavior.

handoff_summary.completed: Created `tests/helpers.py` exporting `FakeRunner` (full stub with results/proposal/writes/workdir tracking) and `OrchestratorTests` (setUp + tearDown: temp git repo + RepositoryStorage initialization). Updated `test_orchestrator.py` to replace the inline class definitions with `from helpers import FakeRunner, OrchestratorTests as _OrchestratorBase` and a `class OrchestratorTests(_OrchestratorBase)` wrapper containing all original test methods. All subclasses (`DeleteBeadTests`, `DeleteBeadCliTests`, `StructuredHandoffFieldsTests`, `BeadAutoCommitTests`) continue to inherit from `OrchestratorTests` without changes. Both files pass Python syntax checks.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. The import relies on pytest's default "prepend" import mode adding the `tests/` directory to `sys.path`, making `helpers` importable without a `tests/__init__.py`. If pytest is ever reconfigured to use `importlib` import mode, a `tests/__init__.py` would need to be added. The three other test files (`test_model_override.py`, `test_config_wiring_phase3.py`, `test_config_wiring.py`) have their own local `FakeRunner` variants with slightly different signatures — they are NOT updated to use the shared harness, as they are out of scope for this bead.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run test suite to confirm no regressions from the extraction.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. Only `tests/helpers.py` (new file) and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` (refactored) were modified. No source files touched.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The `OrchestratorTests` class in `test_orchestrator.py` contains hundreds of test methods that must remain in that file (they are concrete tests, not just base infrastructure). Extracting setUp/tearDown to `helpers.py` under the name `OrchestratorTests` and re-declaring the class in `test_orchestrator.py` as `OrchestratorTests(_OrchestratorBase)` was chosen over: (a) moving all tests to helpers.py (wrong semantics for a helpers module), (b) naming the base something else in the export (acceptance criteria requires `OrchestratorTests` as the exported name). The alias `_OrchestratorBase` avoids name collision in the local scope while satisfying the export contract. Import uses bare `from helpers import ...` rather than `from tests.helpers import ...` because there is no `tests/__init__.py` — pytest's default "prepend" import mode adds the test directory to `sys.path`, making `helpers` importable directly.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) all tests in `test_orchestrator.py` still run and pass after the extraction; (2) `FakeRunner` and `OrchestratorTests` are importable from `tests/helpers.py`; (3) subclasses `DeleteBeadTests`, `DeleteBeadCliTests`, `StructuredHandoffFieldsTests`, `BeadAutoCommitTests` still pick up setUp/tearDown correctly from the base chain.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The three other test files with local `FakeRunner` definitions (`test_model_override.py`, `test_config_wiring_phase3.py`, `test_config_wiring.py`) were NOT migrated to use the shared harness. Their `FakeRunner` variants differ slightly (no `proposal_value` or different `backend_name`) and are not covered by this bead's scope.

conflict_risks: Low. Only `tests/helpers.py` (new file) and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` (refactored) were modified. No source files touched.

execution_history.summary: Extracted `FakeRunner` and `OrchestratorTests` (setUp/tearDown base) into `tests/helpers.py`. Updated `test_orchestrator.py` to import the shared harness with an alias (`_OrchestratorBase`) and re-declare `OrchestratorTests` inheriting from it, preserving all test methods and subclass inheritance chains unchanged.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
